text: Add a short interactive tour that lets users skip steps and return later.
Show a sample project with realistic data before asking for configuration choices.
Offer keyboard shortcuts and a searchable command palette during the first session.
Provide a printable checklist for teams that need approval before setup.
Start with a lightweight sandbox so people can experiment without risk.
Use contextual tooltips only where the interface contains unfamiliar terminology.
Publish benchmark notes that explain where the system performs well and where it fails.
Give administrators a clear audit trail for every generated recommendation.
Show how customer data is isolated, retained, deleted, and exported.
Include deployment stories from different organization sizes and technical environments.
Provide service-level details for uptime, support response, and incident handling.
Make model configuration changes visible in release notes that users can review.
The team should reduce setup friction for non-technical reviewers.
The team should prove that the product works with messy legacy inputs.
The team should clarify ownership of exported reports and project metadata.
The team should document accessibility support for keyboard and screen-reader users.
The team should explain how pricing changes affect long-running projects.
The team should define a migration path for organizations with strict procurement rules.
